title: Top 10 Spanish TV Shows on Netflix You Need to Watch in 2026
metaDescription: The 10 best Spanish TV shows streaming on Netflix in 2026. From La Casa de Papel to Sky Rojo, discover gripping dramas, thrillers, and romances from Spain.
content:
Netflix has become the global gateway to Spanish-language television, and 2026 is no exception. Whether you are a longtime fan of Spanish drama or just discovering the genre, Netflix's library is stacked with some of the best series ever produced in Spain. From heist thrillers to teen mysteries to gritty crime dramas, these 10 shows represent the absolute best that Spanish TV has to offer right now.

1. La Casa de Papel (Money Heist)

Genre: Heist / Thriller | Seasons: 5 | Status: Complete

The show that launched Spanish TV into the global mainstream. La Casa de Papel follows the Professor, a criminal mastermind who recruits eight specialists to pull off the most ambitious heist in history: printing billions of euros inside the Royal Mint of Spain. The first two seasons are among the most gripping television ever made in any language. The non-linear storytelling, the cat-and-mouse dynamic between the Professor and police negotiator Raquel, and the Dali-masked robbers singing "Bella Ciao" became iconic cultural moments. Seasons 3 through 5 expand the scope to the Bank of Spain and raise the stakes even higher. If you have not seen this show, it is the single best place to start with Spanish TV.

2. Elite

Genre: Teen Drama / Mystery | Seasons: 8 | Status: Complete

Set at Las Encinas, an exclusive private school in Spain, Elite blends teen drama with murder mystery in a way that few shows have managed. Three working-class students receive scholarships to the elite institution, and their arrival ignites class tensions, forbidden romances, and eventually, a killing. Each season uses a flash-forward structure centered on a crime, slowly revealing who did what and why. The first three seasons are outstanding, featuring a powerhouse original cast and sharp social commentary on wealth, privilege, and identity. Later seasons rotate in new characters with mixed results, but the early run is essential viewing.

3. Vis a Vis (Locked Up)

Genre: Prison Drama / Thriller | Seasons: 5 (including El Oasis) | Status: Complete

Often called Spain's answer to Orange Is the New Black, Vis a Vis follows Macarena Ferreiro, a naive young woman who ends up in Cruz del Sur prison after taking the fall for her boss and lover's financial crimes. Inside, she encounters Zulema Zahir, one of the most compelling villains in television history. The show starts as a fish-out-of-water prison drama and evolves into something much darker and more complex. The dynamic between Macarena and Zulema is the heart of the series, shifting between rivalry, grudging respect, and something approaching twisted friendship. The spinoff, El Oasis, sends both characters on one final, violent road trip. Raw, intense, and unapologetic.

4. Las Chicas del Cable (Cable Girls)

Genre: Period Drama / Romance | Seasons: 5 | Status: Complete

Set in 1920s Madrid, Las Chicas del Cable follows four women who work as telephone operators at Spain's first national telephone company. The show explores their friendships, ambitions, romances, and struggles for independence in a patriarchal society. Lidia, Carlota, Marga, and Angeles each face their own battles against the constraints of their era, from abusive husbands to family expectations to social norms that demanded women stay silent. The period costumes and sets are gorgeous, and the show does a solid job balancing soapy romance with genuine historical drama. It loses some steam in later seasons, but the ensemble chemistry and feminist themes make it consistently watchable.

5. Alta Mar (High Seas)

Genre: Mystery / Period Drama | Seasons: 3 | Status: Complete

A murder mystery set aboard a luxury transatlantic ocean liner in the 1940s. Sisters Eva and Carolina Villanueva discover that their family's connection to the ship holds dark secrets, and a series of mysterious deaths threatens everyone aboard. Alta Mar combines Agatha Christie-style whodunit plotting with lavish period production design. The confined setting of the ship creates natural tension, and the show makes good use of its closed-room mystery format. It is not as deep as some other entries on this list, but at three tight seasons, it never overstays its welcome. A great weekend binge if you enjoy elegant mysteries.

6. Valeria

Genre: Romantic Comedy / Drama | Seasons: 3 | Status: Complete

Based on the bestselling novel series by Elisabet Benavent, Valeria follows a writer in Madrid struggling with her marriage, her career, and the temptation of a new love interest. The show is often compared to Sex and the City, and the comparison is fair: Valeria and her three best friends (Lola, Carmen, and Nerea) navigate love, ambition, and personal crises with humor and honesty. It is lighter than most shows on this list, but it fills an important gap in the Spanish TV landscape. The Madrid setting is beautiful, the dialogue is sharp, and the friendship dynamics feel authentic. If you want something warm and breezy between heavier dramas, Valeria delivers.

7. Sky Rojo

Genre: Action / Thriller | Seasons: 3 | Status: Complete

From the creators of La Casa de Papel, Sky Rojo follows three women — Coral, Wendy, and Gina — who escape from a forced prostitution ring and go on the run from their pimp, Romeo, and his two enforcers. The show is fast, violent, and visually stylish, with episodes running under 30 minutes that hit like adrenaline shots. The pace is relentless: car chases, close calls, and brutal confrontations in every episode. Beneath the action, the show explores trafficking, exploitation, and survival with more nuance than its breakneck pacing might suggest. Not for the faint of heart, but if you want intensity, Sky Rojo is unmatched.

8. Toy Boy

Genre: Mystery / Drama | Seasons: 2 | Status: Complete

Hugo Beltran, a male stripper in Marbella, is convicted of murder and sentenced to 15 years in prison. When new evidence surfaces, he is released and sets out to prove his innocence and uncover who framed him. Toy Boy is a guilty-pleasure thriller with a twisty plot, a sun-soaked Marbella backdrop, and a cast that leans into the show's campy energy. It is not prestige television, but it is enormously entertaining. The mystery keeps you guessing, the characters are over the top in the best way, and the show does not take itself too seriously. Sometimes you just want a fun, wild ride, and Toy Boy delivers exactly that.

9. Feria: The Darkest Light

Genre: Mystery / Horror | Seasons: 1 | Status: Complete

Set in a small Andalusian town in the 1990s, Feria follows two sisters, Eva and Sofia, whose lives are shattered when their parents are implicated in the ritualistic murders of 23 people in the nearby mines. As the town turns against them, the sisters dig into their parents' past and uncover a secret cult with deep roots in the community. Feria blends mystery, horror, and coming-of-age drama with a distinct visual style and unsettling atmosphere. The Andalusian setting gives it a different feel from the typically Madrid-or-Barcelona-centric Spanish shows. At one season, it is a tight, focused story that does not waste a single episode. Underrated and genuinely creepy.

10. El Internado: Las Cumbres (The Boarding School: Las Cumbres)

Genre: Mystery / Teen Drama | Seasons: 3 | Status: Complete

A reboot of the beloved 2007 Spanish series, El Internado: Las Cumbres is set in a remote boarding school in the mountains where troubled teens are sent by their families. Beneath the school's strict discipline lies a web of ancient secrets, a mysterious religious order, and dangers lurking in the surrounding forest. The show combines teen drama elements with supernatural mystery, and the isolated mountain setting creates a constant sense of unease. It is darker and more atmospheric than the original, and while it does not always stick the landing on its more ambitious plotlines, the setting and mystery elements make it a compelling watch for fans of shows like Yellowjackets or The Society.

How to Get the Most Out of These Shows

A few tips for English-speaking viewers diving into Spanish TV on Netflix:

  • Watch with subtitles, not dubbing. The English dubs on Netflix are serviceable but strip away the performances. The original Spanish voice acting carries emotion and nuance that dubbing cannot replicate. Subtitles let you hear the actual actors.
  • Start with La Casa de Papel or Elite. Both are accessible entry points with fast pacing and hooks that grab you within the first episode. Once you are comfortable with subtitled viewing, branch out to slower-burn shows like Las Chicas del Cable or Feria.
  • Use Netflix's language settings. Switch your Netflix profile language to Spanish (Espanol) and the platform will surface more Spanish-language content in recommendations. This is the easiest way to discover new shows beyond this list.
  • Check regional availability. Not all of these shows are available in every Netflix region. If a title is missing from your library, a VPN can help you access the full Spanish Netflix catalog.

Final Thoughts

Spanish television has earned its place alongside the best TV from any country. The writing is bold, the production values rival anything on American or British networks, and the storytelling does not shy away from difficult topics. Whether you want a heist that keeps you on the edge of your seat, a prison drama with an unforgettable villain, or a romantic comedy set in sunny Madrid, this list has something for you. Start watching. You will not regret it.
author: SpanishTVShows Editorial
